description: Frameworks and tools for classifying data based on sensitivity, regulatory requirements, and business value, enabling appropriate protection controls for each classification level.

classificationLevels.description: Information intended for public disclosure. No confidentiality impact if disclosed.
Information for internal use. Minor business impact if disclosed externally.
Sensitive business or personal information. Significant impact if disclosed.
classificationLevels.controls: Encryption, DLP enforcement, access logging, need-to-know

classificationLevels.description: Highly sensitive information. Severe impact if disclosed or modified.
classificationLevels.controls: Strong encryption, MFA, DLP blocking, DAM, data masking, enhanced logging

approaches.description: Scans file contents using regex, keyword matching, and NLP
approaches.accuracy: High for structured data, moderate for unstructured

approaches.description: Uses metadata, location, and user context for classification
Users manually classify documents with prompted labels
approaches.accuracy: Variable — depends on training and compliance

approaches.description: Machine learning models trained on classified data samples
approaches.accuracy: High when properly trained, improves over time

tools.features: Visual marking, metadata tagging, policy enforcement
Email classification, document marking, DLP integration
Automated discovery and classification, risk-based prioritization

governmentClassification.description: Malaysian government uses the Official Secrets Act 1972 (OSA) classification system

levels.description: Information that would cause damage to national interest
Information that would cause serious damage to national interest
Information that would cause exceptionally grave damage to national interest

malaysianContext.bnmRmit: BNM RMiT S10.4 requires financial institutions to classify data based on sensitivity and apply controls proportional to classification level.
